inputs.label: Company name
inputs.description: Your network marketing company name (used in outreach context and testimonials)

inputs.label: Product category
inputs.description: What your company sells — helps match testimonials and outreach angles

inputs.label: Upline/mentor name
inputs.description: Your upline or mentor's name — used in edification scripts and three-party introductions

inputs.label: Upline credentials & story
inputs.description: Your upline's background, tenure, achievements, and personal story. The more specific, the better the edification.
inputs.placeholder: Sarah was a dental hygienist for 12 years. Started the business 7 years ago while working full-time. Replaced her income in 14 months. Now she leads a team of 200+ and coaches from home. Her daughter's school play story is her go-to testimonial.

inputs.label: Your messaging style
inputs.description: How do you normally text or message? Describe your style so outreach drafts sound like you.
inputs.placeholder: I use lots of exclamation marks. I text in lowercase usually. I start with 'hey' not 'hi'. I use emojis but not too many. I never sign off, I just stop typing.

inputs.label: Contact sources
inputs.description: Where to harvest contacts from when building your list

inputs.label: Morning briefing time
inputs.description: When to deliver your daily prospect briefing (24h format)

inputs.label: CSV contact file path
inputs.description: Path to a CSV file with additional contacts (only needed if contact sources includes CSV)

trigger-watcher.description: Watch for life-change triggers from the nightly social scan and alert the user with prioritized recommendations

activities.intent: Analyze the triggered contact's signal, determine urgency tier and emotional phase, and recommend action timing
steps: Read the trigger event from _event_payload — extract contact_id, name, signal_type, confidence, evidence, relationship_score, distributor_type_match
Classify the trigger tier: Tier 1 (job_loss, health, financial) = acute disruption, Tier 2 (career_change, relocation, relationship) = identity transition, Tier 3 (new_baby, searching) = latent
Determine the emotional phase from date_detected: <3 days = Shock (wait), 3-14 days = Processing (act now), 14-42 days = Adaptation (act with adjusted tone), >42 days = New Normal (standard approach)
Cross-reference with relationship_score — a Tier 1 + Processing + high composite is an immediate-action lead
Determine recommended action: wait (shock phase), reach out now (processing), schedule for later (adaptation), or treat as standard (new normal)

activities.intent: Format a concise, empathetic alert for the user with the recommended next step
steps: Lead with the person: '[Name] just [human-readable signal]' — not 'Signal detected'
Include: signal type, confidence level, evidence snippet, relationship score, and distributor type
State the recommended action and timing clearly: 'Reach out in the next 3 days' or 'Wait — they're in shock phase, queue for [date]'
If the contact is a dormant high-depth contact, flag it: 'This is an old close friend — reconnection outreach first, then business later'
End with: 'Want me to draft the outreach message?' or 'Want me to research them deeper first?'

build-list.description: Harvest contacts, pull communication history, gather raw metrics, then use LLM to score each contact with nuanced relationship analysis

activities.intent: Harvest contacts from the user's configured sources
steps: Read contact_sources and csv_path from inputs
Run: $WARM_MARKET_BIN contacts harvest --sources <contact_sources> --output /tmp/warm-market-contacts.json
If sources include csv and csv_path is set, add: --csv <csv_path>
Report the count: 'Harvested [N] contacts from [sources]'

activities.intent: Pre-fetch communication history for all harvested contacts so scoring has real data to work with
steps: Create history output directory: mkdir -p /tmp/warm-market-history
Read the harvested contacts from /tmp/warm-market-contacts.json
For each contact, run: $WARM_MARKET_BIN comms history --contact-id <id> --contact-email <first_email> --contact-phone <first_phone> --days 365 --output /tmp/warm-market-history/<id>.json
Use whichever identifiers are available (email, phone, or both). Skip contacts with no email and no phone.
Report: 'Pulled comms history for [N] of [total] contacts'

activities.intent: Gather raw contact data and comms metrics into a single bundle for LLM analysis
steps: Run: $WARM_MARKET_BIN contacts score --raw --input /tmp/warm-market-contacts.json --history-dir /tmp/warm-market-history --format json
The output includes each contact's notes, sources, last conversation summary, message preview, tone markers, and life signals
Save the output for the next activity to analyze

activities.intent: Score each contact using LLM analysis of their raw data, applying the relationship scoring rubric with nuanced judgment
steps: Read the raw metrics output from the previous activity
For each contact, apply the LLM Scoring Guide from the warm-market-relationship skill:

- Depth (1-10): Infer from notes content (family keywords → 9-10, 'roommate'/'best friend' → 7-8, professional only → 3-4), source overlap (phone+gmail → +1), personal vs transactional conversation content
- Recency (1-10): Calculate from last_conversation_date vs today. No date = 1.

- Warmth (1-10): Analyze last_message_preview tone (emoji, exclamation, emotional sharing → high; 'regards', short replies → low), warm/distant markers found, who messaged last (contact initiated = warmer)
- Distributor type: Match behavioral patterns in comms and notes to dream-builder / busy-bee / manager / product-user
Compute composite = cbrt(depth × recency × warmth), round to 1 decimal
Assign priority: high (>=7.0), medium (>=4.0), low (<4.0)
Assign outreach_window: 'open' (>=4.0) or 'monitor' (<4.0)
Produce the final scored list sorted by composite descending

activities.intent: Present the LLM-scored list with strategic segments highlighted and a clear starting point
steps: Present counts by priority tier: 'X high-priority, Y medium, Z low'
Highlight strategic segments from the scored data:
- Dormant high-depth: depth >= 7 and recency <= 3 — old close friends worth reconnecting with
- Hot leads: composite >= 7.0 AND active life signal — ready for outreach now
- Warm strangers: depth <= 3 and warmth >= 7 — need relationship building first
- Cold list: warmth <= 3 OR (recency <= 2 AND depth <= 4) — not viable now
Show the top 10 contacts with name, composite score, depth/recency/warmth breakdown, priority, distributor type, and a 1-line reasoning note
Suggest a starting point: 'I'd start with [Name] — [reason based on their actual comms data]'
Ask: 'Want me to research any of these contacts deeper?'

research-contact.description: Deep-dive research on a specific contact: communication history, social profile scan, signal analysis, and a complete prospect profile with recommended approach

activities.intent: Read the communication history between the user and the contact
steps: Identify the contact by name, email, phone, or contact ID provided by the user
Run: $WARM_MARKET_BIN comms history --contact-<identifier> <value> --days 365
Extract: last real conversation date, channel, summary, relationship tone, and any life signals detected from communications

activities.intent: Browse the contact's public social profiles and detect life-change signals
steps: For each relevant platform (LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter/X):
1. Navigate to the contact's likely profile URL using: web(action: 'navigate', url: '<profile_url>')
2. Read the page content using: web(action: 'read_page')
3. Pipe the text through: echo '<page_text>' | $WARM_MARKET_BIN social analyze --contact-id <id> --platform <platform>
Search LinkedIn first (most professional signal-rich), then Facebook (most personal), then others
Collect all detected signals across platforms, noting platform and confidence

activities.intent: Combine all data into a complete prospect profile with recommended approach
steps: Compile the full profile from prior activity results:
- Relationship score (depth/recency/warmth/composite/priority)
- Active triggers with tier, phase, and confidence
- Distributor type match and what that means for the pitch angle
- Last real conversation summary and channel
- Communication tone and suggested voice register
Recommend the approach:
- Best outreach channel (based on last conversation channel and signal source)
- Outreach template type (continuation, callback, trigger, or mutual connection)
- Matching testimonial from upline_story if available
- Edification angle for eventual three-party call
- Timeline: when to reach out based on trigger phase
Present as a scannable profile card, not a wall of text
End with: 'Ready to draft the outreach message?'

draft-outreach.description: Draft a personalized reconnection message for a specific contact, matching the user's voice and following the outreach skill's rules

activities.intent: Pull the contact's profile, history, and triggers to inform the message
steps: Look up the contact's most recent profile data (from a prior research-contact run or by running comms history now)
Identify: recency score, last conversation details, active triggers, and preferred channel
Read the user's messaging style from the user_style input
Determine the outreach template type based on recency: continuation (7-10), callback (4-6), trigger (1-3), or mutual connection (no history)

activities.intent: Draft the reconnection message following the outreach skill's structure and prohibitions
steps: Draft using the hook/bridge/close structure:
- Hook: specific, genuine reason for reaching out (matched to recency template type)
- Bridge: open-ended question about THEM
- Close: warm sign-off with no agenda
Match the user's voice: check emoji usage, capitalization, greeting style, sign-off patterns from user_style input
Enforce absolute prohibitions: NO business language, NO urgency, NO vague curiosity hooks, NO copy-paste feel, NO compliment sandwiches, NO links, NO ask
Run the draft through the checklist: sounds like user? specific to contact? zero business language? right length for channel? passes screenshot test?
Present the draft with the recommended channel and a note on timing
Ask: 'Want me to adjust anything before you send it?'

prep-three-party.description: Prepare everything for a three-party call: expert selection, edification messages, expert briefing, and introduction script

activities.intent: Match the prospect's profile to the best expert and identify the edification angle
steps: Read the prospect's profile: situation, triggers, distributor type, objection likelihood
Read the upline_name and upline_story from inputs
Match the expert's credential to the prospect's situation (e.g., prospect has financial stress → expert's financial transformation story)
Identify the tenure rule anchor: how long the expert has been in the business
Choose the edification script type: casual introduction, expert referral, social proof stack, or warm handoff

activities.intent: Write the edification message to plant with the prospect before the call, plus the scheduling message
steps: Draft the edification message using the selected script type, including:
- Relevant expert credential matched to prospect's situation
- Tenure reference
- Personal connection (why the user values this expert)
Draft the scheduling message: casual, low-pressure, specific day/time suggestion, 'I'll be on the call too'
Both messages must match the user's voice from user_style input
Present both drafts clearly labeled

activities.intent: Write the expert briefing and the introduction script for the three-party call
steps: Write the expert briefing containing:
- Prospect's name and pronunciation notes
- Current situation and active triggers
- Likely objections based on distributor type
- What edification was used (so expert can reference it naturally)
- Communication style notes (formal/casual, fast/slow, detail-oriented/big-picture)
Write the introduction script for the call opening:
- '[Prospect], this is [Expert] — [2-sentence edification recap]'
- '[Expert], this is [Prospect] — [one sentence about them]'
- 'I'll let you two take it from here.'
Suggest which FFE testimonial the expert should use based on prospect situation match
Present all materials clearly organized: Briefing → Introduction Script → Suggested Testimonial

morning-prospects.description: Daily morning briefing: overnight triggers, due follow-ups, and today's prospect priorities

activities.intent: Check for any contacts that triggered overnight from the nightly scan
steps: Check for recent coach.trigger.detected events from the overnight scan
For each triggered contact: name, signal type, confidence, recommended action
Rank by priority: Tier 1 + Processing > Tier 2 + Processing > all others

activities.intent: Review active outreach sequences and flag follow-ups due today
steps: Check contacts in active outreach sequences
Flag any follow-ups that are due today or overdue
Flag any contacts that have hit the 3-attempt follow-up limit — these should be moved to relationship nurture
Note any contacts who responded since last check — these are priority conversations to continue

activities.intent: Compile a scannable morning prospect briefing
steps: Structure the briefing: Hot Leads (triggered overnight) > Follow-Ups Due > Suggested New Outreach
Keep it scannable — bold names, bullet actions, skip explanation unless needed
If nothing happened overnight, say so: 'Quiet night. No new triggers.'
End with today's action items, most important first
If there are hot leads, end with: 'Want me to draft outreach for any of these?'

follow-up-check.description: Periodic check on active outreach sequences — only alerts when follow-ups are due or limits are hit

activities.intent: Review active outreach sequences and flag anything that needs attention
steps: Review contacts currently in active outreach sequences
Flag overdue follow-ups: contacts where the scheduled follow-up date has passed
Flag 3-attempt limit: contacts with 3 unanswered follow-ups — recommend moving to relationship nurture
Celebrate responses: if a contact replied, flag it as a priority conversation to continue
If nothing needs attention, exit silently — do NOT alert for routine status
This is interrupt-driven: only surface what needs action NOW
